results.title: Shoji — Wikipedia
results.snippet: Source-domain: woven kumiko laths + translucent washi paper; jigumi grid types.
results.judge_reason: Wikipedia Shoji article directly covers shoji screens, kumiko laths, washi paper, and jigumi grid - the entire source-domain mechanism.

results.title: Shoji Paper Screens
results.judge_reason: Covers shoji screen, washi paper, and light diffusion - directly matches three source-domain content words.

results.title: What Is Shoji Paper — Johnny Tromboukis
results.snippet: Source-domain: washi (kozo/mitsumata/gampi fiber); diffuse transmission property.
results.judge_reason: Addresses washi paper composition (mulberry fibers) and diffuse light transmission - matches mechanism description.

results.title: Why Linear Interpretability Works: Invariant Subspaces (2026/2)
results.snippet: LLM-side: features communicated through linear interfaces must occupy invariant subspaces; structural architectural consequence.
results.judge_reason: Invariant subspaces in LLM features directly relate to subspace orthogonal complement and residual-stream channel splitting concepts.

results.title: Language-Reasoning Disentanglement
results.snippet: LLM-side: residual regression + subspace projection achieves near-orthogonal separation between lexicon, syntax, meaning, reasoning.
results.judge_reason: Directly describes residual subspace orthogonal separation between syntax/structural and semantic/meaning channels - core mechanism analog.

results.title: LLMs Encode Semantics in Low-Dimensional Linear Subspaces (2025/7)
results.snippet: LLM-side: semantic representations compress into compact regions, linearly separable clusters.
results.judge_reason: Semantic encoding in linear subspaces supports the orthogonal subspace split decomposition concept central to candidate.

results.title: PrivacyRestore (2024/6)
results.snippet: LLM-side: privacy-span removal + restoration vectors at inference time.
results.judge_reason: Cited in novelty_claim as a contrast - related privacy mechanism but does not decompose residual stream into structural/content channels.

results.title: Collaborative Obfuscation Privacy LLM Inference (2026/3)
results.snippet: LLM-side: privacy framework on heterogeneous compute.
results.judge_reason: General privacy obfuscation framework but does not address the orthogonal channel-split decomposition - cited as contrast in novelty claim.
